channel.title: AI Unfiltered
channel.description: Chinese AI • Open Source • Security • Incidents. Signal, not noise.

item.title: Beihang-MIT Wearable Robot Helps Children with Muscular Dystrophy Stand Independently
item.description: A wearable robot developed jointly by Beihang University and MIT, weighing just 0.96 kilograms, has helped six children with muscular dystrophy achieve independent sit-to-stand transitions for the first time, earning a feature as Nature's front-page story.

item.title: Bingbi AI Open-Sources BitCPM-CANN: 1.58-bit Training Achievable on Domestic Compute
item.description: Bingbi AI has open-sourced BitCPM-CANN, a complete training framework enabling 1.58-bit model training on domestic AI accelerators, reportedly reducing inference memory requirements by up to six times compared to full-precision training.

item.title: LimX Intelligence Launches LimX Luna: A Full-Size Humanoid Robot for 298,000 RMB
item.description: LimX Intelligence, a Shenzhen-based embodied AI unicorn, has launched LimX Luna — a full-size interactive humanoid robot standing 160cm tall with 27 degrees of freedom, priced at 298,000 RMB for the domestic retail market.

item.title: Geke Semiconductor's 50MP Image Sensors Cross 100 Million Shipments Milestone
item.description: Geke Semiconductor has cumulative shipped over 100 million units of its 50-megapixel image sensor products, with these high-resolution sensors now accounting for a significantly larger share of the company's mobile CIS revenue.

item.title: Elevated errors for Claude Code in Slack
item.description: May 26, 01:56 UTC Investigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: Slow build start time in Workers Builds
item.description: May 25, 17:14 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Customers with builds that timed out can retry their build in the Dashboard May 25, 16:44 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. May 25, 16:36 UTC Identified - The issue has been identified and a...

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Linux Flaws, Defender 0-Days, Router Botnets, and Supply Chain Chaos
item.description: Monday recap. Same mess, new week. A sketchy dev tool got people pwned, old bugs came back from the dead, and security products somehow needed protecting from themselves. A bunch of companies spent the week checking old boxes and forgotten servers they should've patched years ago. Good times....

item.title: Ghost CMS Vulnerability Exploited to Hack Over 700 Websites
item.description: Sites belonging to major universities such as Harvard and Oxford, as well as DuckDuckGo, have been compromised in the attack. The post Ghost CMS Vulnerability Exploited to Hack Over 700 Websites appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Netherlands Seizes 800 Servers, Arrests 2 for Aiding Cyberattacks
item.description: Authorities in the Netherlands have arrested the co-owners of two related Internet hosting companies for operating IT infrastructure used by Russia to carry out cyberattacks, influence operations and disinformation campaigns inside the European Union. The two men were the focus of a 2025...

item.title: Oncology Institute Discloses Data Breach
item.description: The affected third-party vendor has not been named, but one possible candidate is TriZetto. The post Oncology Institute Discloses Data Breach appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Ghost CMS CVE-2026-26980 Exploited to Hijack 700+ Sites for ClickFix Attacks
item.description: Threat actors are exploiting a recently disclosed critical security flaw in Ghost CMS to inject malicious JavaScript code with an aim to fuel ClickFix attacks. According to QiAnXin XLab, the activity involves the exploitation of CVE-2026-26980 (CVSS score: 9.4), an SQL injection vulnerability in...

item.title: The Alert Firehose Finally Meets Its Match
item.description: Ask a cybersecurity pro about Network Detection and Response (NDR) and you might still hear "Noisy," "Too much data." But ask the teams running NDR that includes agentic AI capabilities and you'll hear they're actually using it to catch threats earlier, triage faster, and chase fewer false...

item.title: 266,000 Affected by Data Breach at Radiology Associates of Richmond
item.description: Threat actors stole files containing names and protected health information from the healthcare organization’s systems. The post 266,000 Affected by Data Breach at Radiology Associates of Richmond appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Anthropic: Mythos Detected 23,000 Potential Vulnerabilities Across 1,000 OSS Projects
item.description: Many findings have been confirmed to be critical or high-severity vulnerabilities and the number will continue to increase. The post Anthropic: Mythos Detected 23,000 Potential Vulnerabilities Across 1,000 OSS Projects appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Honor Robot Phone: A Smartphone That Literally Reaches Out and Takes Photos for You
item.description: At Qualcomm's annual Snapdragon Fans event in Shenzhen on May 24, Honor unveiled the Robot Phone — the world's first smartphone to incorporate a motorized arm that doubles as an integrated gimbal, paired with on-device embodied AI for autonomous photography and smart home control.

item.title: Laravel-Lang Packages Poisoned for Malware Delivery
item.description: Published within a 15-minute window, the malicious tags introduced backdoors to exfiltrate CI secrets. The post Laravel-Lang Packages Poisoned for Malware Delivery appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Elevated error rates on Opus 4.7
item.description: May 25, 10:39 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. May 25, 10:32 UTC Monitoring - We had an elevated error rate on Opus 4.7 from 6:30 UTC until 10:30 UTC

item.title: Lazarus Deploys RemotePE Memory-Only RAT Against Financial and Crypto Firms
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have shed light on a cross-platform malware called RemotePE that has been put to use by the North Korea-linked Lazarus Group in attacks targeting financial and cryptocurrency organizations. RemotePE, per NCC Group subsidiary Fox-IT, is part of a multi-stage attack chain...

item.title: TrapDoor Supply Chain Attack Spreads Credential-Stealing Malware via npm, PyPI, and CratesIO
item.description: A new coordinated cross-ecosystem software supply chain attack campaign has targeted npm, PyPI, and Crates.io to distribute credential-stealing malware. The campaign, codenamed TrapDoor, spans more than 34 malicious packages across over 384 versions. The earliest activity was recorded on May 22,...

item.title: BOHM: Zero-Cost Hierarchical Attribution for Compound AI Systems
item.description: arXiv:2605.22866v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Compound AI systems route tasks through hierarchies of specialised components. Attribution is dominated by Shapley-based methods (SHAP), which decompose a coalition value function into per-component marginal contributions and require evaluation of the...

item.title: NeuroNL2LTL: A Neurosymbolic Framework for Natural Language Translation of Linear Temporal Logic
item.description: arXiv:2605.22874v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Effectively translating between natural language (NL) and formal logics like Linear Temporal Logic (LTL) requires expertise that limits formal verification's reach in safety-critical development. Template-based approaches sacrifice expressiveness for...

item.title: RMA: an Agentic System for Research-Level Mathematical Problems
item.description: arXiv:2605.22875v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present $\textbf{Research Math Agents (RMA)}$, an agentic framework for automated reasoning on research-level mathematical problems. Unlike prior studies centered on competition mathematics or formal theorem proving, RMA targets research-level...

item.title: SciAtlas: A Large-Scale Knowledge Graph for Automated Scientific Research
item.description: arXiv:2605.22878v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The exponential growth of global academic output has confronted researchers and AI agents with an unprecedented ``information explosion,'' where fragmented and unstructured knowledge organization impedes deep interdisciplinary integration. Current...

item.title: Energy per Successful Goal: Goal-Level Energy Accounting for Agentic AI Systems
item.description: arXiv:2605.22883v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Current AI energy benchmarks measure consumption at the granularity of a single model invocation or training run. For classical single-turn workloads this unit remains coherent. For agentic systems - where a single user goal may trigger multi-step...

item.title: Latent Cache Flow: Model-to-Model Communication Without Text
item.description: arXiv:2605.22863v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: LLM agents today communicate via text, which incurs considerable latency and information loss due to the need to autoregressively decode the sharer model's state and encode at the receiver model. Recent work such as Cache-to-Cache (C2C; Fu et al.,...

item.title: Reading Calibrated Uncertainty from Language Model Trajectories
item.description: arXiv:2605.22864v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The maximum softmax probability (MSP) represents a default approach when evaluating uncertainty quantification for language model generation with structured output. Although cheap, it is often miscalibrated. Methods that probe the model's internal...

item.title: FusionSense: Tri-Stage Near-Sensor Learning for Runtime-Adaptive Multimodal Edge Intelligence
item.description: arXiv:2605.22868v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Autonomous systems and smart-industry deployments increasingly split computation across near-sensor, edge, and cloud resources, where tight energy, latency, and reliability budgets demand run-time adaptivity. In practice, deciding what to compute and...

item.title: FuRA: Full-Rank Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning with Spectral Preconditioning
item.description: arXiv:2605.22869v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Both full fine-tuning (Full FT) and parameter-efficient fine-tuning methods such as LoRA introduce weight updates without accounting for the spectral structure established during pretraining. As a result, noisy gradients from limited fine-tuning data...

item.title: The Readout Shortcut: Positional Number Copying Dominates Arithmetic CoT Readout in Small Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2605.22870v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Chain-of-thought (CoT) prompting is necessary for arithmetic in small language models, yet shuffling its steps preserves most performance. What does CoT contribute if not logical sequencing? In three 1-3B instruction-tuned LMs on GSM8K, we isolate the...

item.title: Evaluating Large Language Models in a Complex Hidden Role Game
item.description: arXiv:2605.22826v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Quantifying the deceptive potential of Large Language Models (LLMs) is critical for AI safety, yet difficult to achieve in uncontrolled environments. This work investigates the reasoning, persuasion, and deceptive capabilities of LLMs within the...

item.title: A Survey of Text and Speech Resources for Hausa and Fongbe: Availability, Quality, and Gaps for NLP Development
item.description: arXiv:2605.22828v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This survey provides a comprehensive catalog of publicly available text and speech resources for two West African languages: Hausa, an Afroasiatic language with approximately 80-100 million speakers, and Fongbe, a Niger-Congo language spoken by...

item.title: Query-Adaptive Semantic Chunking for Retrieval-Augmented Generation: A Dynamic Strategy with Contextual Window Expansion
item.description: arXiv:2605.22834v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems depend critically on document chunking quality for retrieving relevant context. Fixed chunking segments documents into uniform units irrespective of semantics or user intent, producing a precision-recall...

item.title: Knowledge Distillation for Low-Resource Open-source Text-to-SQL Model
item.description: arXiv:2605.22843v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Text-to-SQL converts natural language questions into executable SQL queries, enabling non-technical users to access relational databases for analytics and intelligent data services. In real-world scenarios, performance is often constrained by...

item.title: How Far Will They Go? Red-Teaming Online Influence with Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2605.22880v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: As large language model (LLM)-based agents increasingly participate in online discourse, red-teaming their capacity to support political influence campaigns is critical for information integrity. In pursuit of this goal, we focus on locally deployed...

item.title: Harness, Scaffold, and the AI Agent Terms Worth Getting Right
API Errors
item.description: May 23, 19:41 UTC Investigating - We are investigating elevated error rates for API requests. Customers may experience failures when making API calls.

item.title: Cloudflare Zero Trust Gateway Issues — FedRAMP High
item.description: May 23, 19:37 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. May 23, 19:35 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. May 23, 19:29 UTC Identified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented. May 23, 19:25 UTC Investigating - Cloudflare is...

item.title: Intermittent errors with app installation token authentication
item.description: May 23, 19:32 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Thank you for your patience and understanding as we addressed this issue. A detailed root cause analysis will be shared as soon as it is available. May 23, 19:32 UTC Update - This is fully mitigated, we will continue to monitor to ensure...

item.title: [AINews] All Model Labs are now Agent Labs
item.description: a quiet day lets us tie together a few quotes as all model labs become agent labs

item.title: Towards Speed-of-Light Text Generation with Nemotron-Labs Diffusion Language Models
Worker Pages Post-Deployment Errors
item.description: May 22, 20:55 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Customers experiencing 5xx errors with Pages deployments should redeploy to resolve. May 22, 20:49 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been released customer experiencing 5xx errors on their pages deployments should redeploy to resolve. May 22,...

item.title: Audit Log Delays
item.description: May 22, 17:33 UTC Identified - The cause of this issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented. May 22, 16:55 UTC Investigating - Cloudflare Audit Log processing is running behind, causing a delay in timely delivery of audit data. This impacts v2 audit logs. v1 is unaffected.

item.title: Increase in users hitting Codex rate limits
item.description: Status: InvestigatingWe are investigating the issue for the listed services.Affected components Codex Web (Degraded performance) CLI (Degraded performance) App (Degraded performance) VS Code extension (Degraded performance) Codex API (Degraded performance)

item.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
item.description: Lawmakers in both houses of Congress are demanding answers from the U.S. Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) after KrebsOnSecurity reported this week that a CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and a vast trove of other agency secrets on a public GitHub...

item.title: Specialization Beats Scale: A Strategic Variable Most AI Procurement Decisions Overlook
The Download: coding’s future, the ‘Steroid Olympics,’ and AI-driven science
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Anthropic’s Code with Claude showed off coding’s future—whether you like it or not At Anthropic’s developer event in London this week, Code with...

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude Opus 4.7
item.description: May 22, 10:31 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. May 22, 10:26 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. May 22, 10:17 UTC Investigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: Google I/O showed how the path for AI-driven science is shifting
item.description: During Tuesday’s Google I/O keynote, Demis Hassabis, the CEO of Google DeepMind, proclaimed that we are currently “standing in the foothills of the singularity.” It was a striking statement—the singularity is the theoretical future moment when AI rapidly exceeds human intelligence and dramatically...

item.title: The Enhanced Games fit right in with the rest of 2026’s longevity vibes
item.description: This Sunday, a group of 42 athletes will gather in Las Vegas to compete in a somewhat unusual sporting competition. Participants in the inaugural Enhanced Games are being encouraged to take performance-enhancing drugs. The goal is to “push the boundaries of human performance.” The games’ organizers...

item.title: Score-Based One-step MeanFlow Policy Optimization
item.description: Diffusion and flow matching have emerged as expressive policy classes in reinforcement learning, but their reliance on multi-step denoising imposes substantial computational overhead at inference time, which is particularly problematic in online RL. MeanFlow offers a promising alternative by...
